markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Implement backend save/load APIs with EventLedger receipts and conflict-aware revision checks. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: DocumentSaveLoadApi leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentSaveLoadApi is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

evidence.finding: Non-atomic save+index: post-commit error path returned 400/500 for an already-committed save; pre/post-save schema-version parse inconsistency
evidence.fix: All post-commit steps (receipt, backlink rebuild, embed sync) across save/create/import/repair/rename/move are best-effort and RECORDED (receipt_error/backlinks_error/embeds_error + tracing), never an error for a committed write; one pre-commit tree parse drives validation AND indexing (schema-version inconsistency removed)
evidence.tests: knowledge_documents_api_tests 6 passed incl. mt149_committed_save_never_errors_when_post_commit_steps_fail (drops backlink/embed/ledger tables for real; save commits 200 with all three failures recorded; durable v2 reload proven)

findings.title: Non-atomic save+index: post-commit error path returns error for an already-committed save
findings.evidence: api/knowledge_documents.rs:441-449 save commits (UPDATE + version row + receipt), THEN 466-496 re-parses for backlinks; a parse failure returns 400 (line 473) and a backlink-persist failure returns 500 - both for a save that already persisted. Client retry then hits a stale expected_version -> 409.
findings.fix: Either fold backlink rebuild into the same storage transaction as the version bump, or never surface index-step failures as save failures (the code already treats Index permission denial as non-fatal at 493-495; extend that to parse/persist failures).

findings.title: Schema-version inconsistency between pre-save validate and post-save re-parse
findings.evidence: line 436 validates tree with DOCUMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION; line 468 re-parses with saved.schema_version. Divergent docs would validate twice under two versions.

missing_tests: test: a save whose backlink-rebuild step fails must not report the save as failed (committed save + index error)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-149 (DocumentSaveLoadApi) requires backend save/load APIs with EventLedger receipts and conflict-aware revision checks on PostgreSQL authority. The code delivers a real Axum save/load surface backed by a single-transaction optimistic-concurrency UPDATE (stale expected_version -> 409 Conflict), append-only version history, EventLedger receipts, and a committed-save atomicity law, all proven by route-level integration tests against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL.
findings: save_document handler (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_documents.rs:955-1088): validates block tree + embed targets fail-closed BEFORE commit, then save_knowledge_rich_document_version commits; all post-commit steps (receipt, backlinks, embeds, index) are best-effort and recorded, never erroring a committed write. This is exactly the MT-149 'save/load API w/ receipts' + 'conflict-aware revision checks' behavior.

Optimistic concurrency is genuinely PG-native (src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/knowledge.rs:5128-5207): one tx, UPDATE ... SET doc_version = doc_version + 1 WHERE rich_document_id=$1 AND doc_version=$2; on zero rows it distinguishes stale-version (StorageError::Conflict -> 409) from missing doc (NotFound -> 404), inserts an append-only knowledge_rich_document_versions row, clears the draft, and tx.commit(). Real conflict-aware revision check.

EventLedger receipt path is real (knowledge_documents.rs:425-452): NewKernelEvent built with aggregate knowledge_rich_document and KnowledgeRichDocumentSaved type, persisted via db.append_kernel_event into the real kernel_event_ledger table. Matches current spec 2.3.13.11 (write/promotion leaves a receipt; backend-nav identity headers required).

Prior adversarial-v2 findings are actually fixed in commit aa0ce3f6: committed-save no longer returns 400/500 on post-commit index/parse/persist failure, and the pre/post-save schema-version parse inconsistency is removed (one pre-commit tree drives both validation and indexing, knowledge_documents.rs:968-1069).

Runtime proof is real, not mock: tests/knowledge_documents_api_tests.rs drives the actual routes over loopback against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (knowledge_pg_support.rs: managed_postgres cluster, isolated schema, full migration chain, explicit NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback). mt149_committed_save_never_errors_when_post_commit_steps_fail (lines 1077-1164) DROPs the real backlink/embed/kernel_event_ledger tables, then asserts the save commits 200, records all three errors, and the v2 write is durable on reload.

Migrations 0280-0282 (knowledge_document_identity/embeds/backlinks) are present in src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/. No SQLite, no Docker, no external daemon, no placeholder/todo!/unimplemented! reachable from the save/load path.

Aside (non-blocking, expected): packet cites spec bundle v02.193/anchors at v02.192 while current is v02.194; section 2.3.13.11 resolves identically in the current bundle. Pure paperwork pointer drift, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: git show --stat aa0ce3f6, b506d832; log --grep MT-149

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
